risks.description: service "auth-service" is a synchronous dependency of 6 components and is not marked replicated; its failure takes down every dependent path
risks.mitigation: Run the service with multiple replicas behind a load balancer, add health probes, and verify dependents degrade gracefully (timeouts, fallbacks) when it is unavailable.

risks.description: service "cart-service" is marked stateful but references no data store; in-process state is lost on every restart, scale-in, or node failure
risks.mitigation: Externalize state to a managed data store (or make the service stateless and push state to the client/cache). Stateful in-memory services cannot scale horizontally or survive restarts.

risks.description: data store "users-db" is classified "pii" but is not marked encrypted at rest
risks.mitigation: Enable encryption at rest (platform-managed or customer-managed keys) and enforce encryption in transit; confirm key rotation and access auditing meet the relevant compliance regime.

risks.description: high-criticality service "order-service" synchronously depends on 4 services; a slowdown in any of them propagates to this critical path
risks.mitigation: Introduce timeouts and circuit breakers on each synchronous call, and convert non-essential dependencies to asynchronous events so the critical path can complete degraded.

risks.description: data store "analytics-db" is accessed by 3 components; shared stores couple deployment, scaling, and failure domains and erode service ownership
risks.mitigation: Give the data store a single owning service; other components access it through that service's API or via published events, not direct connections.

risks.description: high-criticality service "auth-service" declares no resilience controls (retry, circuit_breaker, timeout, bulkhead)
risks.mitigation: Add explicit timeout, retry-with-backoff, and circuit-breaker policies on outbound calls; isolate critical work with bulkheads so one slow dependency cannot exhaust shared resources.

next_steps: Run the service with multiple replicas behind a load balancer, add health probes, and verify dependents degrade gracefully (timeouts, fallbacks) when it is unavailable.
Externalize state to a managed data store (or make the service stateless and push state to the client/cache). Stateful in-memory services cannot scale horizontally or survive restarts.
Enable encryption at rest (platform-managed or customer-managed keys) and enforce encryption in transit; confirm key rotation and access auditing meet the relevant compliance regime.
Introduce timeouts and circuit breakers on each synchronous call, and convert non-essential dependencies to asynchronous events so the critical path can complete degraded.
Give the data store a single owning service; other components access it through that service's API or via published events, not direct connections.
risk_rating: Severe risk; the architecture needs rework before it is operable
summary: Architecture risk report for ecommerce-order-platform: 8 services analyzed, 6 risks identified, risk-posture score 7/100.
